title: Tuckerization
content:
{{Top|real}} {{Update|[[Star Wars: The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]}} {{Citation}} {{Redlink}} '''Tuckerization''' (or '''tuckerism''') refers to the act of using a person's name for a fictional entity in a story as an [[Wikipedia:In-joke|in-joke]]. The term is derived from [[Wikipedia:Wilson Tucker (writer)|Wilson Tucker]], a pioneering [[Wikipedia:United States|American]] [[Wikipedia:Science fiction|science fiction]] writer and editor, who made a practice of using his friends' names for minor characters in his stories. Tuckerization is generally for wink-and-nod homages: characters with suspiciously similar names or features, or in the case of ''[[Star Wars]]'', often the actors portraying the characters. It should not be confused with the direct inclusion of real people in fiction, a form of story that is usually called biographical fiction when it is the main focus. The following is a list of instances of tuckerization within the ''Star Wars'' franchise. ==Both continuities== ===Characters=== * [[Adnama]] {{C|[[Adnama/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Amanda Lucas]]) *[[Ar'alani]] {{C|[[Ar'alani/Legends|Legends]]}} (Ara Roselani, a friend of [[Timothy Zahn]]){{WebCite|url=https://www.aralani.com/admiral-aralani|text=Why "Aralani"? - Aralani Photography | Portland Wedding Photographer|archivedate=20230625122322}} *[[Jad Bean]] {{C|[[Jad Bean/Legends|Legends]]}} (Jad Bean){{WebCite|url=http://www.jadbean.com/sergeant-jad-bean-an-officially-named-character-in-return-of-the-jedi.html|text=There is a character in Return of the Jedi named "Sergeant Jad Bean".|work=[http://www.jadbean.com jadbean.com]|archivedate=20210124090502}} *[[Olana Chion]] {{C|[[Olana Chion/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Dominique Chionchio]]){{Blog|thefragrantwookiee|44|Olana Chion: A 'What's the story?' Story}} *[[Jeremoch Colton]] {{C|[[Jeremoch Colton/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Jeremy Bulloch]]){{InsiderCite|90|Refresher Reading}} *[[Roron Corobb]] {{C|[[Roron Corobb/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Ron Cobb]]) *[[Lott Dod]] {{C|[[Lott Dod/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Wikipedia:Trent Lott|Senator Trent Lott]] and [[Wikipedia:Christopher Dodd|Senator Christopher Dodd]]){{BookCite|author=Taylor, Chris|title=How Star Wars Conquered the Universe: The Past, Present Future of a Multibillion Dollar Franchisen|publisher=Basic Books|date=2014|isbn=0465089984|quote=Anyone sitting down to write a screenplay on November 1, 1994, as Lucas apparently did, would have been interrupted eight days into the writing process by one of the most seismic midterm in postwar American history. Republicans took the House and the Senate for the first time in 40 years. A resurgent GOP under House Speaker Newt Gingrich started pushing its tax-cutting, regulation-slashing 'Contract with America.' Democrats, whose messaging had improved since Ted Kennedy's 'Star Wars' flub, started calling it a 'Contract On America.'
"It was perhaps no co-incidence, then, that Lucas started writing about a 'Trade Federation,' aided and emboldened by corrupt politicians, embroiled in some sort of dispute over the taxing of trade to the outlying star systems. We never learn what the dispute is about — whether the Trade Federation was pro- or anti-tax. But what we know is that the name of the leader of the Trade Federation — never actually spoken in the movie, but noted in the script from the start — was Nute Gunray. By 1997, when the GOP Senate leader was Trent Lott, Lucas named the Trade Federation's representative in the Galactic Senate: Lott Dodd. We're a long way from the subtlety of his [George Lucas's] [North] Vietnam metaphor here.}} *[[Cin Drallig]] {{C|[[Cin Drallig/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Nick Gillard]])''[[Star Wars: The Lightsaber Collection]]'' *[[Eeusu Estornii]] {{C|[[Eeusu Estornii/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Sue Rostoni]]){{JCFCite|url=posts/31762425|board=Literature|thread=Sue Rostoni Retiring on July 2011|user=SueRostoni|date=2011-06-14|time=3:14 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200406052330/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/sue-rostoni-retiring-on-july-2011.31755274/page-3}} *[[Kalyn Farnmir]] {{C|[[Kalyn Farnmir/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Mary Franklin]]){{Blog|Yossarian|3|Kalyn Farnmir and Romeo Treblanc - Better Late Than Never}} *[[Dannl Faytonni]] {{C|[[Dannl Mot Notrab is backwards-speak for Tom Barton, Chris's six-year-old son.|archivedate=20100811180151|nolive=1}} *[[Esu Rotsino]] {{C|[[Esu Rotsino/Legends|Legends]]}} (Sue Rostoni){{Blog|listing=true|eeusuestornii}} *[[Silya Shessaun]] {{C|[[Silya Shessaun/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Lisa Shaunessy]]){{Blog|hedecga|5|Silya Shessaun: In-Depth}} *[[Kiera Swan]] {{C|[[Kiera Swan/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Keira Knightley]] and [[w:c:pirates:Elizabeth Swann|Elizabeth Swann]]){{SW|url=series/clone-wars/the-box-trivia-gallery|text="The Box" Trivia Gallery}} {{C|Slide 2}} *[[Nicanas Tassu]] {{C|[[Nicanas Tassu/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Nic Anastassiou]]) *[[Meena Tills]] {{C|[[Meena Tills/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Tina Mills]], former [[Lucasfilm]] image archivist){{SW|url=news/much-to-learn-you-still-have-10-things-you-might-not-know-about-mon-calamari|text=Much to Learn You Still Have: 10 Things You Might Not Know about Mon Calamari}} *[[Coleman Trebor]] {{C|[[Coleman Trebor/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Rob Coleman|Robert Coleman]]){{HBCite|67}} *[[Mar Tuuk]] {{C|[[Mar Tuuk/Legends|Legends]]}} (Tuuk, [[Dave Filoni]]'s cat){{SW|url=series/clone-wars/to-catch-a-jedi-episode-guide|text="To Catch a Jedi" Episode Guide – ''The Clone Wars''}} *[[R2-KT]] {{C|[[R2-KT/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Katie_Johnson|Katie Johnson]]){{SW|url=news/remembering-katie-inspiring-hope-with-r2-kt-art-part-2|text=Remembering Katie, Inspiring Hope with R2-KT Art, Part 2}} ===Locations=== *[[Bardotta]] {{C|[[Bardotta/Legends|Legends]]}} ([[Wikipedia:Brigitte Bardot|Brigitte Bardot]]){{YouTube|video=eVxXU_kQVAU|text=Everything Star Wars with J. This is awesome. Thanks for the shout out Adam!|archivefile=File:Neco_Jeyrroo_FB.png}} *[[RazBohan]] (Baz McAlister and Rohan Williams, hosts of the ''Force Material'' podcast){{Twitter|mikechenwriter|status/1527314055244836864|[[Mike Chen (author)|Mike Chen]]|quote=#31 The RazBohan long-range communicator is named after @bazmcalister and @rohan_williams, hosts of the excellent @ForceMaterial podcast. FM covers behind-the-scenes inspirations of George Lucas, it's great for sci-fi history buffs.
